How Escrow Payment Works on 360How Escrow Payment Works on 360 App Online payment in Nigeria can be risky when there is no trust between the buyer and the seller. Many people are afraid to send money to vendors, service providers, or people they meet online because they do not know if the product will be delivered or if the service will be done properly.
This is why escrow payment is important.
Escrow payment simply means that money is kept safely until both sides complete the agreement. The buyer does not send money directly to the seller immediately. Instead, the money is held safely while the seller delivers the product or the service provider renders the service.
360 App uses this idea to help Nigerians buy, sell, and pay online with more confidence.
You can visit 360 here: https://360.com.ng/
What Is Escrow Payment?
Escrow payment is a safe payment method where money is held by a trusted system until the transaction is completed.
In simple words, escrow protects both the buyer and the seller.
For the buyer, it means the money will not be released immediately until the product is delivered or the service is rendered.
For the seller or service provider, it means the buyer has already shown payment commitment before the work is completed.
This creates trust between both parties.
Instead of sending money blindly to someone online, escrow makes the transaction more secure and organized.
What Is 360 App?
360 App is a digital platform that helps people connect, chat, buy, sell, and transact safely.
It is built for buyers, sellers, vendors, freelancers, artisans, business owners, and service providers who want a better way to do business online.
With 360, users can discover people, products, and services. They can chat with the person they want to do business with, agree on what they want, and make payment safely.
The main value of 360 is trust.
It helps reduce the fear of online scams by making sure payment is handled in a safer way.
How Escrow Payment Works on 360 App
Escrow payment on 360 App works in a simple way.
First, the buyer finds a seller, vendor, business, freelancer, artisan, or service provider on 360.
Second, the buyer starts a chat with the person. This allows both sides to discuss the product or service clearly.
Third, both parties agree on the price, delivery time, product details, service details, and expectations.
Fourth, the buyer makes payment through the secure payment process.
Fifth, the money is kept safe and is not released immediately to the seller or service provider.
Sixth, the seller delivers the product or the service provider renders the service.
Seventh, the buyer confirms that the product has been delivered or the service has been completed.
After confirmation, the money can then be released to the seller or service provider.
This process makes online transactions safer because the buyer is not paying blindly, and the seller knows the buyer is serious.
Why Escrow Payment Is Important on 360
Escrow payment is important because it reduces fear in online transactions.
In the traditional way of buying online, a buyer sees a product, chats with the seller, collects account details, sends money directly, and waits for delivery. If the seller is honest, everything may go well. But if the seller is fake or unserious, the buyer may lose money.
With escrow payment on 360, the process is safer.
The buyer can chat, agree, and pay safely. The money is not released until the product is delivered or the service is rendered.
This helps reduce problems like fake vendors, payment without delivery, poor service, wrong product delivery, and sellers disappearing after payment.
How Escrow Protects Buyers : —
Escrow protects buyers by making sure their money is not released immediately without delivery or service completion.
Before paying, the buyer can chat with the seller or service provider. The buyer can ask questions, confirm the product, discuss the price, agree on delivery, and make sure everything is clear.
After payment, the money is kept safe until the seller completes the agreement.
This gives the buyer more confidence.
The buyer does not have to send money directly to a stranger and hope for the best. Instead, the buyer follows a safer process that helps protect the transaction.
For buyers, escrow payment on 360 helps reduce the fear of being scammed.
How Escrow Protects Sellers and Service Providers
Escrow payment does not only protect buyers. It also protects sellers and service providers.
Many genuine sellers lose customers because buyers are afraid to pay online. Even when the seller is honest, the buyer may not trust the process.
With escrow payment, buyers may feel more comfortable because the transaction is safer. This can help sellers build more trust and close more sales.
Escrow also shows that the buyer is serious. If a buyer makes payment through a secure process, the seller knows the buyer has committed to the transaction.
For service providers, this is very useful. A customer can agree to a service and make payment, while the service provider completes the work. Once the work is completed and the customer is satisfied, the money can be released.
This creates balance between the customer and the service provider.
Escrow Payment for Products on 360
Escrow payment can be useful when buying products online.
For example, a buyer may want to buy clothes, shoes, phones, gadgets, beauty products, food items, home items, or other products from a vendor.
Instead of sending money directly to the vendor, the buyer can chat with the seller on 360 and confirm the product details. They can agree on the price, delivery cost, delivery time, size, color, quantity, and other important information.
After the agreement, payment can be made safely.
The seller delivers the product.
When the buyer receives the product and is satisfied, the money can be released.
This makes product buying safer and more trusted.
Escrow Payment for Services on 360
Escrow payment can also be useful for services.
Many Nigerians pay for services like phone repair, cleaning, tailoring, beauty services, delivery, website design, graphic design, digital marketing, tutoring, event services, and other local services.
The problem is that customers may be afraid to pay before the service is completed. At the same time, service providers may not want to start work if the customer has not shown payment commitment.
Escrow helps both sides.
The customer can pay safely, and the money is kept secure. The service provider then renders the service. Once the customer confirms that the service has been completed properly, the money can be released.
This makes service payment safer and more professional.
360 Escrow Payment vs Direct Bank Transfer
Direct bank transfer is common in Nigeria, but it can be risky when dealing with someone you do not know.
With direct bank transfer, once the buyer sends money to the seller, the seller has full control. If the seller refuses to deliver, delays the product, sends the wrong item, or blocks the buyer, the buyer may have little protection.
With escrow payment on 360, the process is different.
The money is not released immediately. It is kept safe until the seller delivers or the service provider completes the work.
This makes escrow payment safer than sending money directly to an unknown seller.
Why Chat Before Escrow Payment Matters
Before making escrow payment, it is important to chat and agree clearly.
Many online problems happen because buyers and sellers do not understand each other properly before payment.
A buyer may think delivery is free, while the seller expects delivery payment. A customer may expect a full service, while the service provider understands only part of the job. A buyer may expect a new product, while the seller is selling a used one.
360 helps reduce this problem by allowing both parties to chat before payment.
The buyer and seller can discuss everything clearly before the money is held.
This makes the transaction smoother and safer.
Who Can Use Escrow Payment on 360?
Escrow payment on 360 can be useful for many people.
Buyers can use it when buying products online.
Sellers can use it to build trust with customers.
Vendors can use it to make customers feel safer.
Service providers can use it to work with serious customers.
Freelancers can use it to handle client payments more safely.
Artisans can use it to connect with customers and receive jobs with more trust.
Business owners can use it to make their transactions more professional.
Why Nigerians Need Escrow Payment
Nigerians need escrow payment because online business is growing fast, but trust is still a major problem.
More people are buying online.
More people are selling online.
More people are hiring service providers online.
But many people are still afraid of being scammed.
Escrow payment helps reduce this fear.
It gives buyers more confidence, helps sellers build credibility, and gives service providers a better way to work with customers.
Simple Way to Understand Escrow on 360
The simple way to understand escrow payment on 360 is this:
You chat with the seller or service provider.
You agree on the product or service.
You pay safely.
The money is kept secure.
The seller delivers the product or renders the service.
You confirm that you are satisfied.
The money is released.
This process protects both sides and makes online transactions safer.
Download and Use 360
You can learn more about 360 through the official website:
https://360.com.ng/
You can download 360 on Google Play:
https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.app.busylife&hl=en&pli=1
You can also download 360 on the Apple App Store:
https://apps.apple.com/us/app/360-simplify-your-life/id6756823776
Final Thoughts
Escrow payment on 360 App helps make online transactions safer in Nigeria.
It protects buyers by keeping money secure until the product is delivered or the service is rendered. It also helps sellers and service providers build trust with customers and deal with more serious buyers.
Instead of sending money directly to someone online without protection, 360 gives users a safer way to chat, agree, pay, deliver, confirm, and release money.
For buyers, it reduces fear.
For sellers, it builds trust.
For service providers, it creates a more professional payment process.
If you want a safer way to pay for products and services in Nigeria, escrow payment on 360 App is a smart option to consider.
